items.headline: [MEDIUM 6.3] filelock is a platform-independent file lock for Python

content.excerpt_text: filelock is a platform-independent file lock for Python. content.excerpt_text: The `time_calibrators` crate attempted to exfiltrate `.env` files to a server that was in turn impersonating the legitimate `timeapi.io` service. The malicious crate had 1 version published on 2026-03-03 approximately 3 hours before removal and had no evidence of actual downloads. There were no crates depending on this crate on crates.io. Rust security response working group thanks cybergeek for finding and reporting this, and thanks to Emily Albini for co-ordinating with the crates.io team.

items.headline: changedetection.io has Reflected XSS in its RSS Tag Error Response

content.excerpt_text: A reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the `/rss/tag/` endpoint of changedetection.io. The `tag_uuid` path parameter is reflected directly in the HTTP response body without HTML escaping. Since Flask returns `text/html` by default for plain string responses, the browser parses and executes injected JavaScript. items.headline: Craft CMS has unauthenticated activation email trigger with potential user enumeration

content.excerpt_text: The `actionSendActivationEmail()` endpoint is accessible to unauthenticated users and does not require a permission check for pending users. items.headline: neqo-qpack has iInteger overflow in qpack dynamic table indexing

content.excerpt_text: ### Summary An unsanitized qpack index can lead to an integer overflow, panicing in debug mode, accessing the wrong or no dynamic table entry in release mode. What does this mean for Firefox? Firefox runs Neqo in release mode. A malicious remote can cause its own QUIC connection to fail to use qpack, i.e. compression, or enter an inconsistent state.
